ucs.title: [LLM] Agrius (Pink Sandstorm / Agonizing Serpens) ASPXSpy web shell — IIS w3wp.exe spawning recon shells after public-app exploit
ucs.description: Hunts post-exploitation activity from the Agrius web shell chain: after exploiting a public-facing app (Fortinet, Exchange, Liferay, SysAid), the actor drops an ASPXSpy/MOIS variant onto the IIS server and operates via cmd.exe / PowerShell child processes spawned by w3wp.exe. This catches the very narrow handover window between T1190 → T1505.003 → T1059.003 that Agrius repeatedly uses against Israeli targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdLines values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","schtasks.exe","vssadmin.exe","plink.exe","procdump.exe","ntdsutil.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","schtasks.exe","vssadmin.exe","plink.exe","procdump.exe","ntdsutil.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"           // IIS worker = web shell host
| where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren)
| extend WebRecon = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("whoami","net user","net group","ipconfig","systeminfo","quser","nltest","tasklist")
| extend WebExfil = ProcessCommandLine has_any (" -hp"," a -p","-r0","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","certutil -urlcache","bitsadmin /transfer")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          AppPoolCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          WebRecon, WebExfil
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","schtasks.exe","vssadmin.exe","plink.exe","procdump.exe","ntdsutil.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                     // process create (4688 audit must be enabled)
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"              // IIS worker = web shell host
| extend ChildExe = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildExe in (SuspiciousChildren)
| extend WebRecon = CommandLine has_any ("whoami","net user","net group","ipconfig","systeminfo","quser","nltest","tasklist")
| extend WebExfil = CommandLine has_any (" -hp"," a -p","-r0","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","certutil -urlcache","bitsadmin /transfer")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, WebRecon, WebExfil
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Agrius's standard playbook against Israeli/ME targets is internet-edge exploitation followed by an ASPXSpy-family web shell (Microsoft / SentinelOne / CheckPoint reporting on Agonizing Serpens 2023, Moneybird 2023, Fox Kitten/Pink Sandstorm overlap). Shell processes spawned by the w3wp.exe app pool worker are extraordinarily rare in legitimate IIS workloads (legit ASP.NET apps almost never invoke

ucs.title: [LLM] Agrius wiper service install — sc.exe creating service with binPath in ProgramData/Temp/Public (Apostle, DEADWOOD, MultiLayer, Moneybird, Bi
ucs.description: Hunts the service-based deployment step Agrius uses for every wiper/ransomware payload it has shipped: Apostle, DEADWOOD, Fantasy, MultiLayer, Moneybird, IPsec Helper, BiBi-Windows are all registered as Windows services with binPath pointing to a writable, non-system directory. Catches T1543.003 just before detonation.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="sc.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*" AND Processes.process="*binpath*" AND (Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Windows\\Debug\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Intel\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Recovery\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "binpath=\\\"?(?<binPath>[^\\\" ]+)" | rex field=process "create\s+\"?(?<svcName>[^\\\" ]+)" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let WriteableStagingPaths = dynamic([
    @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\windows\temp\",
    @"\appdata\local\", @"\appdata\roaming\", @"\perflogs\",
    @"\windows\debug\", @"\intel\", @"\recovery\", @"\$recycle.bin\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "sc.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where cmd has "create" and cmd has "binpath"
| where cmd has_any (WriteableStagingPaths)
// Agrius binary names observed across SentinelOne / Microsoft / CheckPoint reporting
| extend KnownAgriusName = cmd has_any ("apostle","deadwood","fantasy","moneybird","multilayer","bibi","ipsechelper","msupdate","msupdate.exe","sandals","powerless")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ServiceCreateCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent     = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA  = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          KnownAgriusName
| order by Timestamp desc
// Pivot: correlate to the dropper file write
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\")
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".sys"
    | project FileTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, DroppedFile = FolderPath, DroppedSHA256 = SHA256
  ) on DeviceName
| where isnull(FileTime) or abs(datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, FileTime)) <= 30
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let WriteableStagingPaths = dynamic([
    @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\windows\temp\",
    @"\appdata\local\", @"\appdata\roaming\", @"\perflogs\",
    @"\windows\debug\", @"\intel\", @"\recovery\", @"\$recycle.bin\"]);
let ServiceCreate = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688                                     // requires CommandLine auditing
    | where NewProcessName endswith "sc.exe"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
    | where cmd has "create" and cmd has "binpath"
    | where cmd has_any (WriteableStagingPaths)
    | extend KnownAgriusName = cmd has_any ("apostle","deadwood","fantasy","moneybird","multilayer","bibi","ipsechelper","msupdate","sandals","powerless")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, KnownAgriusName;
// Companion signal: 7045 - service installed (catches Powershell New-Service / direct SCM API too)
let ServiceInstalled = Event
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventLog == "System" and EventID == 7045
    | extend EventDataLower = tolower(tostring(parse_xml(ParameterXml)))
    | where EventDataLower has_any (WriteableStagingPaths)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, RenderedDescription, ParameterXml;
union withsource=Source ServiceCreate, ServiceInstalled
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Agrius Wiper Service Install via sc.exe with Non-System binPath
id: 5b3c9f81-2a4d-4e62-9a1b-7e0f8c4d6a91
status: experimental
description: Detects sc.exe being used to register a Windows service whose binary lives in a writeable non-system directory (ProgramData, Users\Public, Windows\Temp, PerfLogs). This is the standard service-installation step used by every Agrius (Pink Sandstorm / AMERICIUM / Agonizing Serpens) wiper and ransomware payload — Apostle, DEADWOOD, Fantasy, MultiLayer, Moneybird, IPsecHelper, and BiBi-Windows.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1030/
  - https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/from-wiper-to-ransomware-the-evolution-of-agrius/
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2023/the-rewards-of-fantasy-iranian-apt-targets-the-diamond-industry/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/04/18/nation-state-threat-actor-mint-sandstorm-refines-tradecraft/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1543.003
  - attack.g1030
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\sc.exe'
  selection_create:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'create'
      - 'binpath'
  selection_path:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Local\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Debug\'
      - '\Intel\'
      - '\Recovery\'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_img and selection_create and selection_path and not filter_machine_account
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Bespoke line-of-business installers placing services under ProgramData (rare; baseline first)
  - Antivirus / EDR self-installers under Public\* or ProgramData\* — allowlist by parent SHA256
level: high
ucs.rationale: Agrius is documented (SentinelOne 'From Wiper to Ransomware' 2021, CheckPoint 'Fantasy' 2023, Microsoft 'Pink Sandstorm/MERCURY' 2022-2023) to deploy each successive wiper as a Windows service with binPath pointing to a writeable directory — Apostle was registered as 'MsUpdate', DEADWOOD as a service in %TEMP%, Fantasy via supply-chain dropper installing services into ProgramData, Moneybird and Mu
